stories.headline: Oil prices rise despite Iran’s proposal to reopen Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: Brent crude rises more than 1 percent despite Iran's offer to reopen waterway in exchange for deferral of nuclear talks.

stories.headline: Iran war live: Trump reviews peace plan; UN calls for Hormuz to reopen
stories.summary: UN chief says US-Iran standoff in the Strait of Hormuz risks triggering a global food emergency.

stories.headline: Trump Is Dissatisfied With Iran’s Plan to Reopen Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: The proposal would have set aside questions about what to do with Iran’s nuclear program.

stories.headline: King Charles III arrives at White House for four-day US visit
stories.summary: Britain’s King Charles III and Queen Camilla were greeted at the White House by US President Donald Trump.

stories.headline: Trumps greet King Charles III and Queen Camilla at the White House
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump and First Lady Melania Trump greet Britain's King Charles III and Queen Camilla upon arrival at the South Portico of the White House in Washington, DC.

stories.headline: King Charles III welcomed at the White House as four-day state visit begins
stories.summary: The state visit to mark the 250th anniversary of US independence comes amid a strain in the UK - US special relationship following Trump's repeated bashing of British Prime Minister Keir Starmer over the US war with Iran.

stories.headline: King Charles Will Speak of ‘Reconciliation and Renewal’ During Address to Congress
stories.summary: The state visit of King Charles III comes at a moment of tension over the war in Iran between President Trump and Prime Minister Keir Starmer.

stories.headline: ‘Shortcomings and failures’ could sink Aukus nuclear submarines plan, UK inquiry warns
stories.summary: Australia is dependent upon UK’s ability to deliver new submarines but report says ‘cracks are already beginning to show’“Cracks are already beginning to show” in the UK’s funding for the Aukus agreement that could derail the ambitious nuclear submarine plan, a British parliamentary inquiry has found, highlighting a threat to Australia’s security.UK shipbuilding has been under-funded for decades and the country’s submarine availability is “critic

stories.headline: At least 20 killed in Colombia highway blast
stories.summary: Colombia's President Gustavo Petro has blamed a 'narco-terrorist group', led by a former FARC fighter, for the attack.

stories.headline: EU energy mix: Crisis pushes balance on nuclear, renewables and security
stories.summary: The EU is pivoting back to nuclear after decades of hesitation, driven by the dual shock of the energy crisis and the realisation that renewables alone can't power a heavy industrial economy.

stories.headline: New Zealand spy plane reports possible North Korea sanctions breach at sea
stories.summary: New Zealand's Defence Force says a North Korean ship was observed engaging in a possible transfer of illicit goods at sea.

Spain, France, Portugal: Renewables race heats up as governments scramble to keep energy bills down
stories.summary: "As long as we depend on oil and gas, we will continue to ​pay the price of other people's wars,” said French Prime Minister Sebastien Lecornu.

stories.headline: China State Refiners Seek to Resume Fuel Exports After Halt
stories.summary: China’s state-owned refiners have begun applying for government permits that would allow them to resume fuel exports in May, citing plentiful domestic stockpiles.

stories.headline: UAE Tells Some Oil Buyers to Pick Up Gulf Supply Outside Hormuz
stories.summary: Abu Dhabi National Oil Co. has informed some term customers of the availability of cargoes for loading off Fujairah, outside the Persian Gulf, as producers explore different routes to bring their oil to market.

stories.headline: Horizons Middle East & Africa 4/28/2026
stories.summary: Horizons Middle East & Africa is your daily spotlight on one of the world's fastest-growing regions. Live from Dubai, we bring you the latest global markets and analysis, plus news-making interviews, with a special focus on MEA. All that and more, as you head to the office in the Gulf, pause for lunch in Hong Kong, or start your day in London or Johannesburg.  (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Iran, Rate Calls Loom Over Asia’s Tech‑Led Rally | The Asia Trade 4/28/2026
stories.summary: "Bloomberg: The Asia Trade" brings you everything you need to know to get ahead as the trading day begins in Asia. Bloomberg TV is live from Tokyo and Sydney with Shery Ahn and Haidi Stroud-Watts, getting insight and analysis from newsmakers and industry leaders on the biggest stories shaping global markets. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Yuan Gains Weigh on Chinese Earnings as Forex Losses Mount
stories.summary: Foreign-exchange losses from yuan conversions are weighing on Chinese companies’ profits, underscoring the currency’s strength as an emerging risk for earnings.

stories.headline: Beijing Blocks Meta‑Manus Deal That’s Already Done | The China Show 4/28/2026
stories.summary: “Bloomberg: The China Show” is your definitive source for news and analysis on the world's second-biggest economy. From politics and policy to tech and trends, David Ingles and Avril Hong give global investors unique insight, delivering in-depth discussions with the newsmakers who matter. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Korean Investment Management's Bae on markets
stories.summary: Korea Investment Management CEO Jaekyu Bae cautions investors against focusing solely on memory chips. He shares his outlook on the Korean stock market and outlines the firm’s ETF strategy. He speaks with David Ingles on "Bloomberg: The China Show."  (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Brain-computer interface firm Gestala on growth plans
stories.summary: Phoenix Peng, co-founder and CEO at Gestala, discusses growing his Chengdu-based ultrasonic brain-computer interface company after recently receiving a $21 million angel round. He speaks with Haslinda Amin from the sidelines of the Bank of America Breakthrough Technology Dialogue APAC. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.summary: Taylor Swift is filing to trademark her voice and likeness, apparently to protest against AI misuse. This move comes after a similar legal strategy launched earlier this year by actor Matthew McConaughey.

stories.headline: The purr-fect shot: New book celebrates legendary cat photographer Walter Chandoha
stories.summary: Before there were cat memes, there was Walter Chandoha. The legendary feline photographer's earliest images are homaged in a new book, "Family Cats: From the Archive 1949-1968".

stories.headline: Taxpayer-funded bottom trawling costs Europe billions. Has Türkiye found the solution?
stories.summary: Türkiye’s Gökova Bay shows what happens when bottom trawling is banned. What can other European countries learn?

stories.headline: Ukraine summons Israel's ambassador over allegedly stolen grain being shipped to Haifa
stories.summary: Kyiv has warned Israel of a brewing diplomatic crisis, should authorities permit a vessel carrying grain allegedly stolen from Russian-occupied Ukrainian territories to dock and unload.

stories.headline: Man charged with attempted assassination of Trump in White House correspondents' dinner shooting

stories.headline: Trump says King will be 'very safe' during US visit after security talks
stories.summary: The state visit will go ahead despite concerns raised after a gunman targeted an event attended by the president.

stories.headline: Cole Tomas Allen, Correspondents’ Dinner Shooting Suspect, Was Propelled by Outrage, Authorities Say
stories.summary: A man who has worked as a tutor and graduated from the California Institute of Technology was charged with trying to assassinate the president after an armed attack at the White House correspondents’ dinner.

stories.headline: Oil Climbs as US Weighs Iran Proposal With Hormuz Still Shut
stories.summary: Oil edged higher as traders weighed the next steps toward peace talks over the Iran war, with the US discussing a proposal from Tehran while the crucial Strait of Hormuz remained almost impassable.

stories.headline: King Charles’s rare state visit offers U.K. a chance to mend ties with Trump
stories.summary: The pageantry began Monday amid heightened security concerns and a growing rift over the Iran war. The U.K. hopes the president’s love of pomp and the king’s “poker face” can help heal their alliance.

stories.headline: Correspondents’ dinner shooting suspect charged with attempting to assassinate the president, two other felonies
stories.summary: In a message sent to family members, the 31-year-old suspect called himself a “friendly federal assassin” and criticized the Trump administration.

stories.headline: Israeli settlers attack Palestinians and set house on fire
stories.summary: Israeli settlers set fire to a Palestinian home in the occupied West Bank village of Jalud.

stories.headline: Colombia offers record $1.4m-reward for rebel it blames for deadly bomb attack
stories.summary: Officials say a rebel leader known as "Marlon" ordered the attack in which 20 people were killed.

stories.headline: Kim Jong Un opens memorial for N Korean soldiers killed in Ukraine war
stories.summary: Kim Jong Un and Russian Defence Minister Andrey Belousov unveiled the memorial in Pyongyang.

stories.headline: King Charles Visits Trump’s Washington After Chaos From Press Dinner Shooting
stories.summary: Presidents use state visits to show off all that is appealing about American culture. But the shooting at the White House correspondents’ dinner has cast a shadow over the visit.

stories.headline: Republicans Push for Trump’s White House Ballroom After Gala Attack
stories.summary: The attack on a press dinner in Washington, which is being called an attempted assassination of President Trump, has also renewed the fight over reopening the Homeland Security Department.

stories.headline: Archbishop praises Pope's anti-war comments during Vatican visit
stories.summary: Dame Sarah Mullally, the first female head of the Church of England, had a private discussion with Pope Leo during the trip.

stories.headline: Investigators in Trump Assassination Attempt Look Through Bluesky for Suspect’s Motive
stories.summary: Federal authorities are looking into whether Cole Tomas Allen posted on Bluesky as “coldforce,” who wrote and promoted liberal views that did not stand out on the left-leaning platform.

stories.summary: The oil sector in the South Caucasus and Central Asia is shifting towards more flexible trading strategies as volatile markets and Asia-led demand reshape global energy flows.

stories.headline: Italy extradites alleged Chinese hacker to US accused of spying for Beijing during COVID-19 pandemic
stories.summary: The FBI accuses Xu Zewei of hacking US government entities, research institutes and universities involved in vaccine research in 2020 and 2021 at the height of the pandemic.

stories.headline: Pirates seize another vessel off Somali coast as threat level increased
stories.summary: The UK's maritime monitor reports at least four suspected piracy incidents in the past week off the coast of Somalia.

stories.headline: Bank Shares Drop After Indian Regulator Tightens Bad Loan Norms
stories.summary: Shares of Indian lenders fell after the regulator tightened rules that will require banks to set aside more capital against potential losses on their loan and credit portfolios.

stories.headline: Oil Rises With Hormuz Shut, Yen Gains After BOJ: Markets Wrap
stories.summary: Oil extended its rally and stocks dropped amid little progress on reopening the Strait of Hormuz. The yen edged higher after the Bank of Japan kept its policy rate unchanged in a split decision.

stories.headline: Hong Kong Airport Taps Local Debt Boom for $1.9 Billion Plan
stories.summary: Hong Kong’s airport operator plans to raise at least HK$15 billion ($1.9 billion) from its only public bond sale this year, according to people familiar with the matter, adding to a surge in debt sales denominated in the city’s currency.

stories.headline: Hongqiao Prices $1.5 Billion Convertible Sale Near Low End
stories.summary: China Hongqiao Group Ltd. priced its 10.2 billion-yuan ($1.5 billion) sale of bonds that can be converted into stock at HK$43.90, near the low end of the marketed range.

stories.headline: Bernie Sanders Pressures Democrats to Reject Super PAC Support in Primaries
stories.summary: The independent senator from Vermont says top Democrats should tell candidates they will otherwise lose access to party resources.

stories.headline: Number of executions in North Korea rose dramatically during Covid – report
stories.summary: Regime used its isolation after closing borders to escalate killings when global scrutiny disappeared, NGO claimsNorth Korea dramatically increased its use of the death penalty after closing its borders during the Covid-19 pandemic, using its isolation to escalate killings when international scrutiny disappeared, according to a report mapping 13 years of executions under the country’s leader, Kim Jong-un.The number of documented cases of executio

stories.headline: EU faces ‘China shock’ as EV imports drive Beijing’s record surplus with bloc
stories.summary: China sold goods worth about $148bn to EU in first quarter of year, but imported just $65bnThe EU is experiencing a prolonged “China shock” as a flood of Chinese EVs into Europe helped push Beijing to a record surplus with the bloc.New data showed China’s trade surplus – where its exports to the EU exceeded imports from the bloc – was $83bn (£61bn) in the first three months of 2026. stories.headline: Netanyahu orders army to 'vigorously attack' Hezbollah in Lebanon
stories.summary: The Israeli PM's directive comes as six people were killed by strikes in Lebanon despite a ceasefire being extended by three weeks.

stories.headline: Israeli strikes kill 14 in Lebanon amid ongoing ceasefire
stories.summary: The US announced three days ago that the ceasefire was extended by three weeks after talks.

stories.headline: Trump cancels US envoys' trip to Pakistan for talks on Iran war
stories.summary: Iran had earlier said there were no plans for a direct meeting with a US delegation led by Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner.

stories.headline: Palestinians in West Bank and some in Gaza vote in local elections
stories.summary: Local elections have been held in the occupied West Bank and in one Gazan city, though Hamas and other groups are not taking part.

stories.headline: BBC visits Chernobyl ghost city 40 years after world's worst nuclear accident
stories.summary: The BBC's Jessica Parker visits Pripyat, which was abandoned in 1986 after an explosion at the nearby Chernobyl nuclear power plant.

stories.headline: Two killed in strike on alleged drug boat in Pacific Ocean, US military says
stories.summary: The incident is the latest in a series of US strikes on alleged drug trafficking boats.

stories.headline: Mexico says US agents killed in crash weren't permitted to operate there
stories.summary: The two Americans who reportedly worked for the CIA died in a car crash after a Mexican-led operation to destroy a drug lab.

stories.headline: Orbán steps down from Hungarian parliament after landslide defeat
stories.summary: The outgoing prime minister will not take up his seat after leading his party back into opposition.

stories.headline: Katya Adler: Europe's Nato allies push back at reported US threat to Spain
stories.summary: Souring relations between Europe and the US have reared their Medusa-like head again, writes the BBC's Europe editor.

stories.headline: What counts as the woods? Judge axes Nova Scotia’s ban that defied ‘commonsense definitions’

stories.headline: Couple discovers Lebanon home destroyed by Israel from satellite image
stories.summary: Joe Elias contacted BBC Verify following reports that his village was among several destroyed by Israeli forces.

stories.headline: Thousands at risk after multi-million dollar Everest flood warning system left to rust
stories.summary: The flood warning system at Imja glacial lake has not been maintained since 2016, fearful locals tell BBC.

stories.headline: Why is this game only legal across Australia one day a year?
stories.summary: Two-up is a coin toss betting game that was played by Australian soldiers during World War One - it’s now only legal across Australia on 25 April.

stories.headline: Headscarf with a beret: Muslim designers showcase floral dresses and boxy streetwear in Paris
stories.summary: Some young attendees say the event demonstrated a more inclusive French culture taking shape.

stories.headline: Israel's Benjamin Netanyahu treated for early-stage prostate cancer
stories.summary: The Israeli prime minister says he is "in excellent physical condition" after having a malignant tumour removed.

stories.headline: Shooting sparks safety fears ahead of World Cup
stories.summary: The Mexican government says the country is safe to visit for the World Cup.

stories.headline: Israeli strikes kill eight Palestinians in Gaza, first responders say
stories.summary: One strike killed three children and two adults near a mosque in the northern town of Beit Lahia on Wednesday evening.

stories.headline: US boards ship carrying Iran oil as Trump threatens mine-laying vessels
stories.summary: Central Command said it has intercepted 33 vessels since imposing a blockade on ships coming to and from Iran.

stories.headline: Lebanon accuses Israel of targeting journalist killed in air strike
stories.summary: Lebanon's prime minister accused Israel of war crimes after IDF attacks on Red Cross vehicles also stopped rescuers from reaching the site.
